spans.text: The hill-tops , where the softest shadows
dreariness on the bleak mountain's head ; No glittering prospect from the upland smiled , The driving squall came dark , the sea heaved wild
the hoar spray of the flashing tide
How changed is now the circling scene
the glancing roofs and white towers
the margin of the lucid bay
the pale headland's height Is touched as with sweet gleams of fairy light
to move With stealing tenderness and grateful love
those who , in yonder dreary mine , Sunk fathoms deep beneath the rolling brine , From year to year , amid the lurid shade , Overwearied , ply their melancholy trade
with varied shade thy hoary brow
That catch the rapt eye of the pausing Muse
he may lift his honest front
His may be Fancy's sunshine , and the Muse
the hard storm , that rends his locks of grey
of the storm that onward sweeps
time sad Nature feels his sway
low sounds moan through the mountain cave
as midnight , When not the lime-leaf moves
the tide's departing roar Alone is heard upon the desert shore , That , as it sweeps with slow huge swell away
It ceases , and the tide's departing roar Alone is heard upon the desert shore , That , as it sweeps with slow huge swell away
fall A Carthage or a Rome
We read upon their base , inscribed by Fame , A Homer's here , or here a Shakespeare's name
that soon may sweep Ourselves unnumbered to the oblivious deep
A Homer's here , or here a Shakespeare's name
all yonder western tract , and this bright bay , Where now the sunshine sleeps , and wheeling white The sea-mew circles in fantastic flight
the curious Muse might love to gaze On the dim record of thy early days
Thought on his native shores of Aradus or Tyre
The noise of battle hurtles on the plain
of battle hurtles on the plain
Behold the dark-haired warriors ! — down thy side , OH
— at dead of night A voice is heard
the cadence of the solemn lay
the holy fane is reared High on the point , where , gazing over the flood , Confessed
graceful the grey tints of Time
the hollow trump that on the ground Is cast
those who traverse the wide main
Scare the grim giant in his murky hall
to this secluded scene they bear Sweets that oft wither in a world of care
Sweets that oft wither in a world of care
the dark terror of thy ancient reign
the stoled spirits of the night
No more thy long and dreary halls affright , Swept by the stoled spirits of the night
the modes of life with slow decay
bliss the sense of human cares
the splendour of thy noontide skies
the splendour of thy noontide skies
The vain pursuits of a degenerate age
engage The vain pursuits of a degenerate age
fain the shade of elder days
the narrow steps , winding with pain , The watch-tower's loftiest platform now we gain

more , or in the storm of night , Or by the glimmering moon's illusive light , Or when the flash , with red and hasty glance , Sudden illumes the sea's remote expanse , The shores , the cliffs , the mountain , till again Deep darkness
end Fitliest our song , and to the shore descend
Fitliest our song , and to the shore descend
the thin flecks of feathery clouds
the thin flecks of feathery clouds
the cry Of the grey gull
we thus from heaven's bright battlements
hear no far-off sounds of earthly ill
no far-off sounds of earthly ill
this seat of care , And ask , if peace , if harmony be there
the clangours and the cries that shake The mad world , and their dismal music make
no more The surge that moans along this mortal shore
